summary: Matched the stage function grInishie1_801FBC4C by introducing a local HSD_JObj* variable for gp->gv.inishie1.blocks[index].jobj2 before passing it to DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP. The stated purpose was to fix register allocation and bring the function to 100% match.
what_changed: In src/melee/gr/grinishie1.c, grInishie1_801FBC4C now assigns gp->gv.inishie1.blocks[index].jobj2 to a local HSD_JObj* named jobj2.
DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP is now called with the local jobj2 instead of the full nested struct/member expression.
The rest of the function behavior remains unchanged: it calls Ground_801C4A08 on the block's hatena_gobj and then clears hatena_gobj to NULL.
smart_moves: Used a minimal source-level refactor, adding only a local temporary, to influence compiler register allocation without changing semantics.
Kept the original data access pattern obvious by naming the temporary jobj2 after the source field gp->gv.inishie1.blocks[index].jobj2.
Targeted a very small unmatched function, making the matching effect easy to review and isolate.
decomp_lessons: When a direct nested field expression passed into a macro does not match, try hoisting the field into a typed local variable to alter register allocation.
For macro calls such as DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP, the exact expression form at the call site can affect generated code even when semantics are equivalent.
Small local temporaries can be legitimate matching tools in stage/ground code when they mirror likely compiler lifetimes.
naming_conventions: Local HSD_JObj* temporaries may use the same semantic name as the struct field, here jobj2 for gp->gv.inishie1.blocks[index].jobj2.
The stage function and file retain the grInishie1 naming prefix used for this ground/stage unit.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced HSD_JObj* jobj2 as a local temporary specifically to fix register allocation.
Replaced DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP(gp->gv.inishie1.blocks[index].jobj2) with DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP(jobj2) to get a 100% match.
No assembly diff details were included beyond the stated register-allocation motivation.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided slice.
The author noted this was a small PR opened to test the waters and asked to be told if anything was missed.
follow_up_queries: Find other DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP call sites where hoisting a nested JObj expression into a local HSD_JObj* may improve matching.
Search grInishie1 functions for similar gp->gv.inishie1.blocks[index] repeated accesses that may need local temporaries for register allocation.
Compare matched stage functions that clear hatena_gobj after Ground_801C4A08 for consistent temporary naming and access patterns.
